summary_of_threads_started_this_week:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community highlight several key proposals aimed at enhancing the network's functionality and security. One notable proposal involves a consensus rule change to establish a perpetual block subsidy by setting an expiration limit on Unspent Transaction Outputs (UTXOs). This plan seeks to extend miner incentives while maintaining the 21 million coin cap, with further details available on [GitHub](https://github.com/bip-proposer/bitcoin-infinity/blob/main/infinity.md). Additionally, new security measures for output types like P2TRv2 are being considered to protect against potential quantum computing threats, with mechanisms such as the Tripwire and Miner Lockdown proposed to disable vulnerable cryptographic paths [source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/5f59804c-6a3b-41e7-9733-6c253353847an@googlegroups.com/T/#m748a7fa3b9c284cae66474304b70d79ba5cd0845).

The community is also exploring user-friendly enhancements, particularly for non-English speakers, through proposals like Daniel's BIP39 recovery phrase improvement, which involves using native-language wordlists as a deterministic overlay for better accessibility [source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/bip39-native-language-display-wordlists-mapped-to-canonical-english/2637). Furthermore, the introduction of the Segregated Data (SegData) method proposes handling non-value data within Bitcoin blocks more efficiently, potentially benefiting applications like timestamping services while ensuring data pruning is optional [detailed discussions](https://github.com/MrHash/bips/blob/4eeeb0afbb9d256d264225801e635d2df1cc875f/bip-segdata.md).

Additionally, advancements in blockchain user interfaces are demonstrated by tools like the ASmap dashboard and BlockSight.Live, which enhance the accessibility and understanding of network activities for both technical and everyday users [ASmap dashboard](https://jorisstrakeljahn.github.io/asmap-dashboard/), [BlockSight.Live](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/roast-our-real-time-bitcoin-explorer-built-for-everyday-bitcoin-users/2654). These developments show a continued focus on improving interaction with blockchain technology, making it more approachable and effective for a diverse range of users.

new_threads_this_week.title: [BIP Proposal] Strict circular economy within the Bitcoin network
new_threads_this_week.summary: - This proposal sets an expiration limit on old Unspent Transaction Outputs.  - Expired satoshis transfer to a pending emission reserve to extend halving epochs.  - The proposal is open for community input and available in detail on GitHub.

new_threads_this_week.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
new_threads_this_week.summary: - Bitcoin development explores security enhancements with P2TRv2 against quantum threats.  - Two mechanisms, Tripwire and Miner Lockdown, have been proposed to improve security.  - These measures increase complexity but bolster network reliability and user participation.

new_threads_this_week.title: BIP39 native-language display wordlists mapped to canonical English
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Daniel proposes enhancing BIP39 for better non-English speaker accessibility.
- His method maintains English mnemonics while adding a native language interface.
- Community feedback is sought to discuss risks and implementation specifics.

new_threads_this_week.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
new_threads_this_week.summary: - SegData introduces a new data storage method in Bitcoin blocks via a soft-fork.  - It allows optional data pruning and places storage costs on opting operators.  - The system supports off-chain data services but not script-evaluated data types.

new_threads_this_week.title: ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network
new_threads_this_week.summary: - The ASmap dashboard, part of the Summer of Bitcoin project, tracks bitcoin-core data changes.  - It features a Network tab that assesses .dat files against observed nodes with a scoring system.  - Developers request feedback on metrics, data clarity, and potential enhancements for future versions.

new_threads_this_week.title: Roast our real-time Bitcoin explorer built for everyday Bitcoin users
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- BlockSight.Live offers a user-friendly Bitcoin blockchain explorer with multilingual support.
- The platform provides live updates, transaction searches, and real-time network visualizations.
- Feedback is encouraged to refine data presentation and enhance user experience.

new_threads_this_week.title: Can Fun, Human-Readable Data Drive Mainstream Adoption? OP_Return revival! Blockonomics new updates referenced
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Blockonomics introduced a text search in Bitcoin transactions for easier user interaction.
- The new feature makes blockchain feel like a personal, permanent time capsule.
- Simple, engaging features in blockchain can enhance understanding and adoption.

new_threads_this_week.title: Backwards compatibility ending for Tor versions <0.4.9 on Sept 1
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- The Tor network's recent update impacts Bitcoin software relying on it.
- The new stable release version 0.4.9.X, launched on February 12, 2026, necessitates upgrades.
- Older versions unsupported after four months could cause operational disruptions.

new_threads_this_week.title: Is CSFS+CAT already a general covenant primitive? Three on-chain demonstrations, and an observation for discussion
new_threads_this_week.summary: - CSFS+CAT effectively mimics traditional blockchain opcodes, enhancing transaction features.  - Demonstrated capabilities include fixed output conditions and novel input management.  - Despite increased script size and costs, these methods enrich signer-involved transactions.

new_threads_this_week.title: A faster Go (golang) secp256k1 library
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- A new Go library for secp256k1 has been developed with dual functionality.
- It leverages CGO for enhanced performance, improving signature verification speed by 350%.
- The library ensures portability across different platforms by using a pure Go fallback.

new_threads_this_week.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Proposed changes aim to enhance HTLC security by eliminating reliance on mempool states.
- Modifications enforce minimum nLockTime and introduce key opcodes to support secure transactions.
- The changes address vulnerabilities and trade-offs, enhancing overall blockchain transaction security.

active_posts_this_week.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts_this_week.summary: - A proposed model enhances blockchain efficiency by incrementally verifying transactions.  - Risks include severe security breaches from not fully validating blockchain history.  - Skepticism exists on adopting UTXO set commitments in Bitcoin due to security concerns.

active_posts_this_week.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Recent attacks on small ECC bit-length curves by advanced hardware emphasize the risks.  - The effectiveness of larger bit-sized curves against such threats is currently debated.  - Cryptographic "canaries" may offer limited but strategic defense against certain attacks.

active_posts_this_week.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts_this_week.summary: - The Bitcoin Development Mailing List discussed enhancing Merkle tree management in blockchain consensus.  - A method named `IsForbiddenMerkleInternalNodePreimage64` was introduced, amid concerns about added complexity.  - Future blockchain headers will extend the timestamp field and require new formats for consistency and security.

active_posts_this_week.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
active_posts_this_week.summary: - The Bitcoin blockchain discussion explores AI agents and contract protocols intersection.  - Recent studies show real-world cryptography like homomorphic encryption needs further refinement.  - Challenges in blockchain for AI-driven contracts require more academic and developmental focus.

active_posts_this_week.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The term "solvable" may be misleading for certain technological challenges.
- Primary concerns focus on privacy versus scaling issues impacting solution feasibility.
- Specificity in debates is crucial to address how these factors influence problem solvability.

active_posts_this_week.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The discussion focuses on changes in cryptographic standards impacting Bitcoin.
- The transition to post-quantum signatures could make block space scarcer.
- There's a strategic focus on preparing Bitcoin for future technological shifts.

active_posts_this_week.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Coding strategies enhance network robustness against DOS attacks compared to targeting nodes.  - Using codes in networks makes disrupting synchronization more difficult.  - Adopting coding methods ensures better protection and stability under malicious attacks.

active_posts_this_week.title: Public archive for Delving Bitcoin
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- A new mirror for delvingbitcoin.org is now operational and accessible online.
- The `discourse-archive` tool archives discussions and its code is available on GitHub.
- The mirroring tool updates frequently but currently does not archive images.

active_posts_this_week.title: Writing Simplicity Programs with SimplicityHL
active_posts_this_week.summary: - The Simplicity programming language has advanced, with a new community forum launched.  - A state commitment mechanism in Taproot enhances contract address connectivity.  - A new covenant, "Third Time's The Charm," ensures secure, conditional fund release.

active_posts_this_week.title: Regular signet reorgs
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- A strategy sets a 25-minute minimum age for blocks to trigger reorganizations.
- Discrepancies in mempool transactions delay mining until manually processed.
- The issue may also affect network performance and transaction fee estimations.

active_posts_this_week.title: Quantum Sunset Economics: a working paper analyzing PACT adoption
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email analyzes security risks in different cryptocurrency transaction types.
- It discusses the increased safety of multisig transactions due to multiple necessary keys.
- A software error is causing incorrect Bitcoin UTXO counts, suggesting the need for classification adjustments.

active_posts_this_week.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- A floor fee is suggested as viable due to compatibility with soft-forking.
- Other proposals include tail emission, fee burn, and priority tipping for balance.
- These methods address transaction fees and blockchain network sustainability.
